task_completion.description: Read the research brief text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal window displaying the contents of 'arctic_anomaly_brief.txt' from the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Open the NOAA OI SST climatology dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A data visualization tool (like Panoply) open with the 'sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc' file loaded, showing the 'sst' (Sea Surface Temperature) variable.

task_completion.description: Create a polar-projected map of Arctic SST for February.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A plot window showing a top-down polar projection (centered on the North Pole). The plot metadata, title, or time controls must indicate that the time step is set to February (e.g., time index 1 or 2 depending on 0-based indexing, or explicitly 'Feb').

task_completion.description: Extract and compare SST values for West Svalbard and the Canadian Arctic at ~78°N.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent viewing the data values at ~78°N using an array view, data probe, or script output. The values should be visible on screen (Canadian Arctic around -2.0°C to -1.5°C, West Svalbard around 0.5°C to 5.0°C) or being typed into the report.

task_completion.description: Export the polar-projected map to the ArcticResearch directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager showing an image file (e.g., 'arctic_sst_february.png') being saved to or existing in '/home/ga/Documents/ArcticResearch'.

task_completion.description: Export the structured text report to the ArcticResearch directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor saving a file or a file manager showing 'spitsbergen_anomaly_report.txt' in '/home/ga/Documents/ArcticResearch'.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI application (like Panoply) to visually create and inspect the map, rather than blindly running a terminal script without viewing the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a graphical data viewer's interface (menus, plot settings, projections) to generate the map.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely extracted the SST values from the dataset rather than hallucinating them.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively probing the map, inspecting the data array, or running a script to print the values for ~78°N before writing them into the report.

integrity.description: The report contains accurate values derived from the dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text report contains values that match the expected ranges (Canadian Arctic: -2.0 to -1.5°C, Svalbard: 0.5 to 5.0°C) and correspond to the data seen on screen during extraction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target month for the map is February. The expected output directory is /home/ga/Documents/ArcticResearch. The expected files are arctic_sst_february.png and spitsbergen_anomaly_report.txt. The extracted SST values must fall within specific ranges: Canadian Arctic SST at ~78°N should be between -2.0°C and -1.5°C, and West Svalbard SST at ~78°N should be between 0.5°C and 5.0°C.
